title: Events, Refs, SHAs, Forks, and Workflow Trust Boundaries · Workflow and Release Recall
summary: An operations handoff reconstructs events, refs, shas, forks, and workflow trust boundaries from delayed run evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the event and revision trust policy under changed load. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for event and revision trust policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive workflow source, checked-out revision, GITHUB_REF, GITHUB_SHA, actor, permissions, and payload from each trigger.
Choose pull request merge, head, or base revision intentionally and state what integration or source claim each can prove.
Model fork approvals, withheld secrets, read-only tokens, compute abuse, contributor changes, and re-run authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust
steps.instruction:
review case — A review maintainer runs fork code through pull_request_target with write authority, then a privileged workflow_run consumes its artifact. Establish workflow source, tested revision, actor, permissions, producer trust, and data-only inspection. Competency probe: Treat workflow_run, issue_comment, repository_dispatch, and downloaded artifacts according to producer identity, revision, permissions, and content trust.

Inspect the Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for treat workflow_run, issue_comment, repository_dispatch, and downloaded artifacts according to producer identity, revision, permissions, and content trust. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust, keep Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.text: For Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A review maintainer runs fork code through pull_request_target with write authority, then a privileged workflow_run consumes its artifact. Establish workflow source, tested revision, actor, permissions, producer trust, and data-only inspection. Competency probe: Treat workflow_run, issue_comment, repository_dispatch, and downloaded artifacts according to producer identity, revision, permissions, and content trust.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: An artifact from a successful low-privilege workflow is safe to execute in a privileged workflow.

stimulus.title: Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review maintainer runs fork code through pull_request_target with write authority, then a privileged workflow_run consumes its artifact. Establish workflow source, tested revision, actor, permissions, producer trust, and data-only inspection. Competency probe: Treat workflow_run, issue_comment, repository_dispatch, and downloaded artifacts according to producer identity, revision, permissions, and content trust.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: An artifact from a successful low-privilege workflow is safe to execute in a privileged workflow.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat workflow_run, issue_comment, repository_dispatch, and downloaded artifacts according to producer identity, revision, permissions, and content trust. Artifact under review: event and revision trust policy.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, copy one familiar workflow, omit event and revision identity, permissions, runner, immutable artifact, failure, rollback, and disposable delivery-environment transfer checks, then accept a green run as production proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
